title: Curry on Carpet
timeEstimate: 30–90 minutes plus multiple fade cycles; often pro
stainChemistry: Same turmeric pigment chemistry as curry-cotton, on carpet. Same ABSOLUTE RULE: NO alkaline cleaners — permanent red shift. Same sunlight + peroxide fade workhorse. Carpet adds pad-contamination risk + patch-test discipline. Fitted carpet loses the sunlight advantage (can't be moved to sun), so fade cycles stretch to days. Curry on carpet trends strongly toward pro-lane — the combination of oil carrier + turmeric pigment + pad + limited sun access is rough at home.
whyThisWorks: Oil carrier off via NSD damp cloth. 3% peroxide damp cloth on residual color. Repeated fade cycles over a day or two. Portable rugs can be sun-fade; fitted carpet substitutes repeat peroxide passes. Honest plateau expectation: aged curry on light carpet often leaves a faint shadow even with pros.
defaultAssumption: Escalate-first posture. Curry on fitted carpet is a hard consumer stain; a pro has better odds than a home user. Portable rugs with sun access = reasonable DIY. Fresh + small = try once before escalating.

spottingProtocol.instruction: ABSOLUTE RULE: do NOT apply baking soda, ammonia, OxiClean, or any alkaline cleaner. Curry's turmeric pigment shifts to permanent DARK RED under alkaline. Set scope honestly: fitted carpet + aged curry often plateaus at faint yellow even with a pro. If the stain is on a portable rug, you have sun access and better odds. Scrape bulk off with a dull blade.

spottingProtocol.technique: Strip oil carrier via damp cloth
spottingProtocol.equipment: NSD in cool water, damp cloth, weighted dry towel
spottingProtocol.instruction: NSD damp cloth. Strips the oil that locks turmeric pigment onto the fiber. Never pour. Weighted dry towel extraction between passes. 2–3 cycles.

spottingProtocol.equipment: 3% H₂O₂ damp cloth, sun if portable
spottingProtocol.instruction: Dab 3% H₂O₂ via damp cloth. For portable rugs: set damp in direct sun 1–2 hours. For fitted carpet: fan-dry and repeat peroxide every 30–60 min over a sunny afternoon. Patch-test first.

spottingProtocol.equipment: NSD damp cloth, heavy dry towel + weight
spottingProtocol.instruction: NSD-damp clean pass. Weighted dry towel 30 min.
Curry is patient-removal. 2–3 peroxide + fade cycles over a sunny day or two. Honest plateau is common — aged curry on light carpet may leave faint yellow.

steps: 2. Cold NSD damp cloth — strip oil carrier.
3. 3% H₂O₂ damp cloth + sun (portable) or repeat fade (fitted).
6. Fitted carpet + aged curry = escalate early.
warnings: Fitted carpet + aged curry = pro territory, set honest expectations

homeSolutions.agent: DON'T Apply Baking Soda or Ammonia
homeSolutions.instruction: Curry's yellow is turmeric. If any alkaline cleaner — baking soda, ammonia, OxiClean, "all-purpose" carpet sprays — touches curry, the yellow permanently shifts to a DARK RED. Use ONLY Dawn + 3% peroxide on curry carpet. Nothing else.

homeSolutions.agent: Consider Calling a Pro — Honest Scope
homeSolutions.instruction: Curry on fitted carpet is hard. If the stain is aged, big, or on light carpet that's hard to patch-test, a carpet cleaner has stronger options. If you're proceeding, expect 2–3 cycles over a sunny day or two and set the expectation that aged curry may plateau at faint yellow.
Lift any solid curry off the carpet with a dull butter knife. Don't rub — drives it into the pad.

homeSolutions.agent: Dawn + Cool Water on a DAMP Cloth
homeSolutions.instruction: Dampen a white cloth with cool water + a drop of Dawn. Press on the spot, lift, rotate. Strips the oil carrier that locks the yellow pigment. Repeat 2–3 cycles. Never pour on carpet.

homeSolutions.agent: Weighted Dry Towel Between Every Wet Pass
homeSolutions.instruction: Press a thick DRY towel with a heavy book on the spot for 20–30 min after each wet pass. Pulls moisture up from the pad.

Dab 3% drugstore hydrogen peroxide on the stain via a damp cloth. Patch-test a hidden area first. Portable rug: lay damp in sunlight 1–2 hours. Fitted carpet: fan-dry, then repeat the peroxide application every 30–60 min over a sunny afternoon.
homeSolutions.safetyNote: Never mix peroxide with chlorine bleach or ammonia.

homeSolutions.agent: Accept Plateau and Escalate If Needed
homeSolutions.instruction: Curry is patient-removal. If 2–3 cycles plateau at faint yellow, that may be the realistic home ceiling. Call a pro if: alkaline was applied (red shift), wool carpet, aged stain on light carpet, or 2 cycles didn't meaningfully fade. Tell them: "Curry on [nylon / wool / synthetic] carpet, age [fresh / overnight / older], home-treated with Dawn + 3% peroxide + sun. [No alkaline / baking soda applied / plateau at faint yellow]."

steps: 1. NO alkaline — baking soda / ammonia / OxiClean all shift turmeric pigment to permanent red.
2. Honest scope check — fitted carpet + aged = often pro.
3. Scrape bulk. Cold water + Dawn on DAMP cloth, 2–3 cycles.
4. Weighted dry towel between wet passes.
5. 3% peroxide + sunlight (portable rug) OR repeat peroxide (fitted). Patch-test first.
6. Accept plateau — aged curry often leaves faint yellow.
7. Escalate for alkaline-applied, wool, or plateau.
products: Heavy book or pan for weighted drying
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if alkaline applied, wool carpet, or two peroxide + fade cycles plateau.
warnings: Skip peroxide on colored carpet without patch test

neverDo: NEVER alkaline on curry — permanent red shift
fiberSensitivities: Synthetic face yarn: tolerates NSD, 3% H₂O₂

materialWarnings: NO alkaline cleaners — permanent red shift
Fitted carpet + aged curry = often pro

escalation.when: Alkaline cleaner applied (red shift), wool carpet, aged curry on light fitted carpet, or two peroxide + fade cycles plateau.
escalation.whatToTell: "Curry on [nylon / wool / synthetic] carpet, age [fresh / overnight / older], home-treated with Dawn + 3% H₂O₂ + [sun / repeat fade cycles]. [No alkaline / baking soda applied / plateau]."

contextQuestions: Has any alkaline cleaner been applied? (critical)
Carpet fiber — nylon / olefin / polyester / wool?
Portable rug (can sun-dry) or fitted carpet?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Same turmeric pigment rules as mustard + curry-cotton. Alkaline-avoid absolute. Carpet adds pad-protection constraints. Fitted carpet plateau is common; honest expectation-setting is part of the protocol.
